NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED OVERRULING DEMURRER AND DENYING MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY PLAINTIFF COLOTTO, LINDA , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSORS-IN-INTEREST TO THE ESTATE OF KENNETH COLOTTO COLOTTO, JOSEPH , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSORS-IN-INTEREST TO THE ESTATE OF KENNETH COLOTTO

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL AND DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES A FAILURE OF THOSE RESPONSIBLE FOR ATTENDING TO THE BASIC NEEDS OF THE ELDERLY DECEDENT KENNETH COLOTTO. FURTHER, THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT AMOUNT TO RECKLESSNESS IN THE COMMISSION OF SUCH NEGLECT. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DEPENDENT NEGLECT. SECOND, DEFENDANTS' RELIANCE ON PLAINTIFFS' FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 425.13(A) WAS EXPLICITLY REJECTED BY THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT IN A CASE THAT WAS CITED BY DEFENDANTS THEMSELVES. (SEE COVENANT CARE, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 771, 776 ["THE COURT OF APPEAL CONCLUDED SECTION 425.13(A) DOES NOT APPLY (TO PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIMS IN ACTIONS ALLEGING ELDER ABUSE SUBJECT TO HEIGHTENED CIVIL REMEDIES UNDER THE ELDER ABUSE ACT). WE AGREE."].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

Notice Of Motin And Motion To Quash Or, In The Alternative , Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-15-2014 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHRYN STEBNER OF STEBNER AND ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 819-0110 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT R. DEERING OF SUPPLE & CANVEL, LLP, TEL. NO. 366-5533 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL and DIGNITY HEALTH's DEMURRER TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE COMPLAINT and MOTION TO STRIKE - Defendants' Demurrer is overruled. The Complaint sufficiently alleges a failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs of the elderly decedent Kenneth Colotto. Further, the allegations in the Complaint amount to recklessness in the commission of such neglect. Defendants' Motion to Strike is denied. First, Plaintiffs have sufficiently alleged a cause of action for dependent neglect. Second, Defendants' reliance on Plaintiffs' failure to comply with CCP section 425.13(a) was explicitly rejected by the California Supreme Court in a case that was cited by Defendants themselves. (See Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 776 ["The Court of Appeal concluded section 425.13(a) does not apply (to punitive damage claims in actions alleging elder abuse subject to heightened civil remedies under the Elder Abuse Act). We agree."].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Notice Of Motin And Motion To Quash Or, In The Alternative , Motion For Protective Order

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL and DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued on the Court's own motion to September 22, 2014. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/eg
